t_ch: 这一切，你心知肚明。滚出去，我们的土地，可恨的{RUBY_B#悬锋建城者}歌耳戈{RUBY_E#}之子！
t_en: All of this, you know very well. Leave our city, cursed son of {RUBY_B#Kremnos Founder}Gorgo{RUBY_E#}!

t_ch: 哈托努斯，我无意加害你！
t_en: I have no intention of hurting you, Chartonus!

t_ch: 羞辱我，不必——滚出去，滚出奥赫玛！
t_en: Humiliate me, you shall not. Leave. Get out of Okhema!

t_ch: 滚吧！你们的王权，带上！悬锋的印戒，享受染血的荣光，你们自己！
t_en: Get out! Symbol of your sovereignty, take it! Signet of Kremnos, bask in blood-soaked glory, your kind alone!

t_ch: 这…母亲的印戒？
t_en: This is... Mother's signet?

t_ch: 又来了，我就知道…！
t_en: It's here again. I knew it...!

t_ch: 哈！终于发狂，可憎的狗蝇。一同毁灭吧，与你们的疯王，在那战场的旋涡中！
t_en: Hah! Finally gone mad, despicable vermin have. With your mad king, perish together, in that maelstrom of battle!

t_ch: 够了，闭嘴！你根本不是哈托努斯——
t_en: Enough, be quiet! You are not at all Chartonus—

t_ch: ——小心！
t_en: —Look out!

t_ch: 一道狭长的阴影破空而来，贯穿了哈托努斯的幻影……
t_en: A long, narrow shadow pierces through the phantom of Chartonus...

t_ch: …好身手。
t_en: ...Nice move.

t_ch: 彼此彼此。
t_en: You, too.

t_ch: 你是丹恒…本人吗？
t_en: Are you... the real Dan Heng?

t_ch: 同样的问题，回敬给你——
t_en: I could ask you the same—

t_ch: 这次是丹恒的幻影吗……
t_en: Are we up against Dan Heng's apparition this time...?

t_ch: 等等。他的气息不同，十分沉静。
t_en: Wait. He seems calm and composed, unlike the rest.

t_ch: 不错，看来还能沟通。那边的灰发战士，回答我——
t_en: Sane enough to communicate, it seems. Hey, gray-haired warrior. Tell me—

t_ch: ——我在鳞渊境开海前，你对我说的最后一句话是什么?
t_en: —What was the last thing you said to me before I parted the waters at the Scalegorge Waterscape?
Parted the waters...?

t_ch: 这我哪记得？！
t_en: How would I even remember that!?

t_ch: 不像演的，看来是本人。
t_en: Hmm, that sounds like something the real you would say.

t_ch: 你要不还是一枪钉死我吧…
t_en: Ugh. Forget it. Just kill me, alright?

t_ch: 不像演的，看来是本人。
t_en: Hmm, that sounds like something the real you would say.

t_ch: 答对了，记性不错。
t_en: That's right. You have a great memory.

t_ch: 终于找到你们了…不容易。
t_en: It wasn't easy, but I finally found you two...

t_ch: 是本人啊。你此前经历了什么？
t_en: So, you are Dan Heng. What did you go through before meeting us?

t_ch: 只是遇见了些前尘影事，不提也罢。
t_en: Just some memories from my past. Nothing worth mentioning.

t_ch: 意思是，你也看见了自己的过往？
t_en: You mean you saw your past too?

t_ch: 哼…「纷争」的试炼竟如此恶毒啊。
t_en: Hmph... The trial of Strife is a vicious one, alright.

t_ch: 你想到什么了？
t_en: What are you thinking?

t_ch: 恐怕，这试炼不属于某一个人…而是一片供所有挑战者与妄念厮杀的疆场。
t_en: This might not be a single person's trial... but an arena for every challenger to engage their own delusions in a bloody battle.

t_ch: 方才，我也见到一位故人…可白厄从未与他结识。
t_en: I saw someone from my past a while ago... Someone Phainon has never met.

t_ch: 呵…听起来算不得坏事。这倒意味着，我们定能在这里找到白厄了。
t_en: Hmm... That doesn't exactly sound like a bad thing. At least it means that we'll be able to find Phainon here.

t_ch: 但愿这片战场没有想象中那么辽阔……
t_en: We can only hope this battlefield is nowhere as extensive as I imagine it to be...

t_ch: …先循着厮杀声的源头前进吧。
t_en: ...Let's first follow the sounds of battle to their source.

t_ch: 呵，果不其然。这里就是战吼的源头……
t_en: Heh. Just as I thought. This is where the battle cries were coming from...

t_ch: …{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}试炼真正的门户。
t_en: ...The true entrance to {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador's{RUBY_E#} trial.

t_ch: 怎么了，两位？
t_en: What's wrong with you two?

t_ch: 那位战士，之前见过…
t_en: I've met that warrior before...

t_ch: 是格奈乌斯，尼卡多利的化身…
t_en: It's Gnaeus, an incarnation of Nikador...

t_ch: 虽然未曾打过照面，但我也感受到了。毫无疑问，那是只属于「纷争」的杀意……
t_en: I'm seeing him for the first time, but there's no doubt about it. What I'm sensing can only be Strife's bloodlust...

t_ch: {RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}本尊——这片战场的主宰。
t_en: The one manipulating this battlefield is... {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} themself.

t_ch: 「呵，迈德漠斯…你诚然是个目光闪亮的勇士。」
t_en: "Ah, Mydeimos... You are a keen warrior, indeed."

t_ch: 你认得我？
t_en: You know me?

t_ch: 「渎神的王贵，我当然认得你。」
t_en: "The blasphemous prince. Of course I know you."

t_ch: 「我还明白：你们是为那白发战士的魂灵而来。」
t_en: "And I happen to know that you're here for the soul of that white-haired warrior."

t_ch: 那就好说了。把那个窝囊的剑士交出来，让我们尽快结束这场闹剧。
t_en: Great. Let's cut to the chase, then. Hand over that poor excuse for a swordmaster so we can be done with this farce.

t_ch: 「我拒绝。」
「方才一路走来，我认可诸位的勇力。但既然你们妄图践踏败者的尊严，我就无法坐视不理。」
t_en: "I applaud your courage and strength for coming this far. But you have chosen to besmirch the dignity of one who has fallen, and I cannot turn a blind eye to that."

t_ch: 看来一场恶战在所难免了。
t_en: This is gonna be one fierce battle we won't be able to get out of.

t_ch: 看在同行过的份上，行行好吧…
t_en: Won't you go easy on us? We traveled together before, remember...?

t_ch: 落败之人…难道他没能战胜试炼么？
t_en: One who has fallen...? Phainon failed the trial?

t_ch: 「他面对内心至深的恐惧，战斗得十分勇敢，在凶猛的杀戮中战败身陨。所以，我将他接入身后这片沙场中，赐他与勇气、坚韧和牺牲相配的荣耀。」
t_en: "He faced the greatest fear in the depths of his heart and fought with exceptional valor, though he eventually succumbed to the brutal onslaughts. I have since brought him to the rear battleground, and granted him glory worthy of his courage, tenacity, and sacrifice."

t_ch: 「想带走他？可以：拿上铜枪，上前来证明自己的信念与他相配……」
t_en: "You wish to take him away? Sure. Pick up a spear and prove that your resolve can rival his..."

t_ch: 「至于不打算流血的懦夫…现在就滚出我的视线。」
t_en: "Of course... cowards who hope to avoid bloodshed are better off disappearing from my sight this instant."

t_ch: 哼，我对你这可悲疯王的高见不感兴趣……
t_en: Hmph. Your opinions are of no interest to me, mad king...

t_ch: 但既然你想一战，那就来吧，泰坦——让我再度以死运为你合拢双眼。
t_en: If it's a fight you're looking for, then you will get one, Titan. I will put you to rest once more with my curse of immortality.

t_ch: 「不错！你那高尚的母亲也曾用手中长枪，让我领教过同样的气魄。」
t_en: "Commendable! Your noble mother displayed the same spirit with a spear in her hands."

t_ch: …别提她的名字。
t_en: ...Don't you dare speak of her.

t_ch: 「为什么？她为捍卫你的尊严，明知自己将死于毒计，却仍向悬锋先王发起角斗。难道你想说，这一切都不过是她空自劳苦么？」
t_en: "Why not? She challenged the former Kremnos king to a duel, even knowing that it was a deadly trap, all to protect your dignity. Are you suggesting her efforts were for naught?"

t_ch: 她的悲剧，正出自你手——「纷争」的化身——你有何面目谈起她的过去？
t_en: Her tragic death was caused by you, the incarnation of Strife. How dare you talk about her past?

t_ch: 够了，别扯这些没用的。身为「纷争」之神，何不以剑明志，让我们速战速决！
t_en: Enough of this nonsense. You should be wielding your weapon as the god of Strife! Battle me and put a swift end to this!

t_ch: 「呵！你的恨意了然于色，迈德漠斯，这些话想必刺痛了你的心胸。但也正因此，我终于明白了……」
t_en: "Hah! Your hatred is as clear as day, Mydeimos. My words must have stung you deeply. But thanks to them, I have also come to realize..."

t_ch: 「这就是你弑父夺得王权，却任凭印戒沉入冥海，不愿延续悬锋荣光的理由……」
t_en: "This is why you killed your father for the throne, only to let your signet sink to the bottom of the Sea of Souls, refusing to let the glory of Kremnos live on..."

t_ch: 「这就是你陨灭纷争之泰坦，却拒绝火种，延续『纷争』的理由……」
t_en: "This is why you slew the Strife Titan, only to reject the Coreflame, letting Strife continue instead..."

t_ch: 「命运的弃儿，我倒要看看你有没有这个能耐——看看你能否亲手熄灭{RUBY_B#「纷争」}自己的宿命{RUBY_E#}。」
t_en: "O child abandoned by fate, show me what you are capable of. Show me if you have the power to put out the flames of {RUBY_B#Strife}your very own destiny{RUBY_E#}."

t_ch: 我一定会撕开你的胸膛，泰坦…正如我对先王所做的那样。
t_en: I will rip your chest apart, Titan... In the same way I ripped apart that former king.

t_ch: 「哈哈哈，既然你想重蹈覆辙，那就来吧：点燃你恐惧的烈焰，踏上沙场，鏖战至『纷争』的疆界，吞噬我；或迷失其中，让自己战死的遗体得到烤慰！」
t_en: "Hahaha, I see you wish to repeat your mistake. Come at me, then. Ignite the flames of your fears and step onto the battlefield. Take this fierce battle to the boundaries of Strife and consume me, or lose yourself in the fight and watch your defeated corpse burn to ash!"

t_ch: 「但记住了，迈德漠斯：{RUBY_B#悬锋建城者}歌耳戈{RUBY_E#}之子，必将浴血代冠——」
t_en: "But remember this, Mydeimos: The son of {RUBY_B#Kremnos Founder}Gorgo{RUBY_E#} will be crowned in blood—!"

t_ch: 「当你破开我的胸膛，那也正是你登神之时——悬锋的继业者啊，我向你致敬！」
t_en: "The very moment you rip my chest apart shall be the moment of your ascension. To you, the successor of Kremnos!"

t_ch: 现在 你的私人浴宫内
t_en: Now, in your private bath chamber

t_ch: 自{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}一战来，你已许久没有睡得如此酣畅。
t_en: Ever since the battle with {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}, you haven't slept so soundly in ages.

t_ch: 纵然死神来叫你起床，你也将倨傲地告诉它：生命因何而沉睡？因为被窝很温暖……
t_en: Even if Death itself came to rouse you, you'd proudly retort: Why does life slumber? Because the bed is warm...

t_ch: …直到你确实嗅到了，一股来自冥河的寒意。不久前，你好像体会过这种感觉。该不会死神真的找上门了吧？
t_en: Yet, a chill from the River of Souls wafted by, a sensation hauntingly familiar. Could Death truly be at your door?

t_ch: 保险起见，还是先睁开眼……
t_en: Best to open your eyes, just in case...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下，你醒了。
t_en: {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}, you're awake.

t_ch: …我说怎么感觉有股寒意。
t_en: ...No wonder I felt a chill.

t_ch: 是说房间太冷了么？
t_en: Is the room too cold?

t_ch: … 遐蝶，生命因何而沉睡？
t_en: ...Castorice, why does life slumber?

t_ch: …是个很深奥的问题，我恐怕难以解答。
t_en: ...That's a complicated question. I'm not sure I can answer that.

t_ch: …你为什么在这儿？
t_en: ...Why are you here?

t_ch: 恕我不请自来…见你还未起床，只好在一旁静静等待。
t_en: I apologize for arriving uninvited. I saw that you were still asleep, so I took the liberty of waiting nearby.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅大人遣我来向阁下转达：她为二位开拓者备了礼物，希望能当面交付。
t_en: I bring a message from Lady Aglaea. She has prepared a gift for you and Dan Heng, and wishes to present it to you in person.

t_ch: 礼物，我要礼物！
t_en: A gift? Gimme, gimme!

t_ch: 那还等什么？快出发吧！
t_en: What are we waiting for? Let's go!

t_ch: 看起来很有精神呢。那我们走吧。
t_en: I'm glad you're in good spirits. Alright, let's set off.

t_ch: 绝对，是你忘记了！这就是*我们*，见过的！家乡的宝贝！
t_en: You must have forgotten! This is a precious relic from our hometown! We definitely all saw it back then!

t_ch: 缇安，这不是真品。*我们*都知道的，这种易碎的制品，不可能保存完好至今……
t_en: Trianne, this is a counterfeit. We both know it's impossible for something this fragile to have survived all those years in one piece...

t_ch: 什么都有可能！*我们*也很易碎，但都从那段…悲伤的历史中，坚持下来了！你看它的颜色，在光芒下！亮晶晶的…和*我们*记忆里，一模一样！
t_en: Nothing is impossible! We are fragile, too! But we still got through those... those sad times. Look at that color... and how it glitters in the light... It's exactly as we remember it!

t_ch: 为什么，你不愿意买下它呢？要是明天它被别人买走了，*我们*、*我们*可能就再也没机会……
t_en: Why won't you buy it? If someone else gets to it before us, we'll... we'll miss our chance forever...

t_ch: 缇安…别激动，你现在需要好好休息……
t_en: Calm down, Trianne... You need rest.

t_ch: 两位，息怒。如果大人们还想考虑考虑，我可以把它留到明天…不，留到两位考虑好，如何？
t_en: Please, there's no need to fret. If you need more time to make a decision, I can put this aside for you until tomorrow... or until you're ready.

t_ch: 不、不必啦。缇安她只是…她明天就会想明白了，先不打扰你做生意。
t_en: No, no, it's alright. Trianne is just... She'll come to her senses tomorrow. We'll be off first.

t_ch: 缇安只是什么？缇安只是很生气！
t_en: Trianne is just what? Trianne is super mad, that's what!

t_ch: 因为你忘了，你忘记了妈妈！妈妈给*我们*，送过一样的东西！
t_en: You've forgotten... You've forgotten all about Mama! Mama gave us something just like this before!

t_ch: 缇安……
t_en: Trianne...

t_ch: 那…明天见？祝两位明天和好如初……
t_en: Uh... See you tomorrow? I hope you two patch things up soon...

t_ch: 嗯，明天见。也祝你生意兴隆。
t_en: See you tomorrow. May the winds of fortune bless you.

t_ch: 缇宝紧随着生气的缇安离开了……
t_en: Tribbie leaves after Trianne, who is fuming...

t_ch: 请问，刚才缇安大人和缇宝大人在谈些什么？
t_en: Excuse me. If you don't mind, could you tell me what Lady Tribbie and Lady Trianne were discussing just now?

t_ch: 遐蝶小姐，好久不见。唉，说实话，我也有些搞不清楚状况。
t_en: Oh, it's Miss Castorice! It's been a while! Well, honestly, I'm not quite sure myself.

t_ch: 我平素喜欢收集工艺品，前些日子在云石市集上看见有人兜售这个小东西，就出价买了下来，摆在小铺当作装饰品。
t_en: See, I have a little hobby of collecting handicrafts. Some time ago, I found this trinket being sold at the Marmoreal Market and bought it to use as decoration at my stall.

t_ch: 刚才二位大人路过，缇安大人一眼就瞧见了它，嚷着说是什么家乡的宝贝。
t_en: When Lady Tribbie and Lady Trianne walked by earlier, Lady Trianne happened to catch sight of it and promptly insisted that it was a precious memento from her hometown.

t_ch: 但缇宝大人觉得只是仿制品，说要真是雅努萨波利斯的手艺，玻璃制品如此易碎，怎么可能从黄金战争的动荡里幸存下来？
t_en: But Lady Tribbie believed it to be a counterfeit. She said it couldn't possibly be from Janusopolis — the likelihood of such fragile glassware surviving the Chrysos War is simply too low.

t_ch: 不知为何，缇安大人似乎很生气。
t_en: For some reason, Lady Trianne became awfully upset after hearing that.

t_ch: 这样啊……
t_en: I see...

t_ch: 阁下，我有意买下此物…能否请你割爱？
t_en: Madam, would you mind selling this trinket to me?

t_ch: 这倒无妨。但这若真是雅努萨波利斯的遗物，恐怕价值不菲。遐蝶小姐，我毕竟是个商人，您看……
t_en: Certainly. It's just... if it truly is a relic from Janusopolis, it could be worth a small fortune... Miss Castorice, I'm running a business here, after all, so...

t_ch: 我理解，尽管出价吧。
t_en: I understand. Feel free to name your price.

t_ch: 等等，我有异议！
t_en: Not so fast! I object to this!

t_ch: 这位朋友，你又有何高见？
t_en: And what might your concerns be, my friend?

t_ch: 想讹黄金裔？胆子太大了！
t_en: You've got some nerve trying to rip off a Chrysos Heir.

t_ch: 缇宝都鉴定为假，你还想狡辩？
t_en: Tribbie already called it a fake! Are you trying to pull a fast one on us?

t_ch: 这…也罢，那我就依成本价，送遐蝶小姐一个顺水人情吧。希望以后，多多支持我的小店哈。
t_en: I... Fine. I'll sell it to you at cost as a sign of goodwill, Miss Castorice. Just promise to support my business in the future!

t_ch: （沉默着盯着她。）
t_en: (Glare at her in silence.)

t_ch: 这…我要不就依成本价，送遐蝶小姐一个顺水人情吧。希望以后，多多支持我的小店哈。
t_en: Uh... I suppose I can sell it to you at cost as a sign of goodwill, Miss Castorice. Just promise to support my business in the future!

t_ch: 嗯，感激不尽。
t_en: Much appreciated.

t_ch: 谢谢你帮我说话。我平日深居简出，虽说有点积蓄，但…确实不擅长与他人打交道，更遑论购物还价。如果没有你，恐怕是要破费了。
t_en: Thanks for helping me out. I'm not one to venture out often, and while I have some savings, it takes a lot out of me to even talk to people, let alone haggle... Had you not been here, I would've been set back plenty.

t_ch: 幸好遇见了好心人。我平日深居简出，虽说有点积蓄，但…确实不擅长与他人打交道，更遑论购物还价了。换作其他商贩，恐怕是要破费了。
t_en: We're lucky that she has a good heart. I'm not one to venture out often, and while I have some savings, it takes a lot out of me to even talk to people, let alone haggle... Had it been another merchant, I would've been set back plenty.

t_ch: 这件工艺品的真伪并不重要。重要的是它对缇安大人有特别的意义，能让她回忆起故乡。
t_en: The authenticity of the piece isn't important. What matters is that it holds special meaning for Lady Trianne and reminds her of her hometown.

t_ch: 我平日受缇安大人诸多照顾，一直想找机会给缇安大人备点薄礼以示谢意，只是不知要送些什么好。今天碰巧撞见此事，也算命运暗中相助，就选它做礼物吧。
t_en: I've long wished to show my gratitude for Lady Trianne's care, but I haven't been able to come up with any ideas for a suitable gift. This chance encounter today feels like divine intervention. Perhaps this is the gift I've been searching for.

t_ch: …只是，这小玩意确实十分易碎。我打算回去后稍作加工，把它缝装到羊毛毡里，这样缇安大人再活泼也不用担心打碎它了。
t_en: Still, this trinket is admittedly rather fragile. I will have to make some adjustments and stitch it onto wool felt to protect it. That way, no matter how much Lady Trianne runs around, it won't break easily.

t_ch: 终于来了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: You're finally here, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 欢迎，蝶，还有来自天外的…「开拓者」。
t_en: Welcome, Cas. And you as well, Trailblazer from beyond the sky.

t_ch: 丹恒，你起得真早。
t_en: You're up so early, Dan Heng!

t_ch: 是你睡太晚了。不过休息充足也是好事。
t_en: You would've been, too, if you hadn't stayed up so late. But what matters is that you got a good rest.

t_ch: 这称呼倒是让人意外。
t_en: I didn't expect you to call me that.

t_ch: 刚才，是你这位沉稳的同伴告诉我这名字背后的意涵。
t_en: Your companion here kindly explained the meaning behind the name to me earlier.

t_ch: 听闻各位在天界外兼行{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}与{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}之途，我想唯有如此直言相唤，方能略表敬意。
t_en: Through him, I've come to understand that you and your friends walk the paths of {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#} and Janus in places beyond the sky. Addressing you appropriately is the least I can do as a show of respect.

t_ch: 呱，礼物，我要礼物！
t_en: The gift! Gimme the gift!

t_ch: …让你们见笑了。
t_en: ...I'm sorry about my companion's behavior.

t_ch: 看到{NICKNAME}先前经历苦战，现在能如此精神，令人欣喜。
t_en: Not at all. It's good to see {NICKNAME} bouncing back with such energy after that tough battle.

t_ch: 奥赫玛将两位卷入{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}一战，令你们身处险境，实属无奈。我等对两位的「开拓」义举感激不尽，愿以奥赫玛最上等的礼仪相谢。
t_en: For all that has happened, it was never our intention to drag you two into the battle against {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} and jeopardize your safety. To express our gratitude for your kind Trailblazing endeavors, we would like to present you with the highest honor of Okhema.

t_ch: 此物名为「神血蜜露」，据传仅有十二瓶，乃是用众泰坦各自神血浇灌的作物酿成。世间仅存的三瓶现皆已收入奥赫玛宝库。
t_en: This is the Dew of Divine Blood, brewed from crops nourished by the blood of the Titans. It is said that only twelve bottles existed. The last three bottles are now being kept in the treasure vault of Okhema.

t_ch: 尚显生机的时代里，但凡众城邦有幸起获之，无一例外将其视作宝藏，在邦交仪式中启封更象征着无上礼遇。
t_en: Back in the days of prosperity, cities fortunate enough to acquire it regarded it as one of the greatest treasures ever. Unsealing it during a ceremony held in honor of two cities' diplomatic ties was the highest form of respect one could show the other.

t_ch: 如今，奥赫玛愿依传统为天外来客斟饮奉赠，以表诚意。
t_en: And now, in keeping with tradition, Okhema dedicates this drink to our esteemed guests from beyond the sky in appreciation of what you have done for us.

t_ch: 好贵重，拿着的手微微颤抖…
t_en: My hand is trembling under the weight of this gift...

t_ch: 我年纪还小，喝不了这个。
t_en: I'm not of drinking age yet.

t_ch: …列车有规定，就是我也不能喝。
t_en: ...The Express has house rules about drinking, and even I'm not exempt to them.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅女士赠以如此贵重之物，恐怕不只是为了表达谢意吧？
t_en: This precious gift isn't simply a token of gratitude though, is it, Lady Aglaea?

t_ch: 你很敏锐，丹恒阁下。两位先前的作为已证明了自己的善意——我们愿以这樽神血蜜露为证，与开拓者们浅结盟谊。
t_en: Very perceptive, Mr. Dan Heng. Your heroic deeds have proven that you come in good faith. With the Dew of Divine Blood as testament, we wish to forge an alliance with the Trailblazers.

t_ch: 恕我直言…与黄金裔结盟，是否意味着我们会被卷入奥赫玛的内部纠葛？
t_en: With all due respect... won't forging an alliance with the Chrysos Heirs embroil us in the conflicts within Okhema?

t_ch: 你应该能理解我们的谨慎源自何处，阿格莱雅女士。
t_en: I'm sure you can understand where our misgivings come from, Lady Aglaea.

t_ch: 确实需要慎重考虑。
t_en: Right, we need to think about this first.

t_ch: 呱，礼物，我的礼物…
t_en: Waaaah, my gift! My gift...

t_ch: 看不出来，你还真的喜欢。
t_en: ...Wow, you seriously took a liking to the gift.

t_ch: 请别误会，在能力允许的前提下，我们仍会尽可能为逐火之旅提供帮助。
t_en: Having said that, Lady Aglaea, please don't misunderstand, we will still do what we can to assist you in the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: 只是…我听说黄金裔与奥赫玛的元老院之间存在分歧，作为外来者，我们不想在这场斗争中站队……
t_en: It's just... I've heard of the friction between the Chrysos Heirs and the Council of Okhema. As outsiders, we are wary of getting caught up in this conflict—

t_ch: 呀，灰宝和丹宝还真是见微知著！很有加入「昏光庭院」的潜力喔。
t_en: Wow, Grayie and Dannie have sharp eyes indeed! You'd make outstanding members of the Twilight Courtyard!

t_ch: 不过，放心吧～光从阿格莱雅女士的微表情和语调就不难判断，她对两位十分真诚呢。
t_en: No need to worry, by the way. I can tell from Lady Aglaea's tone of voice and micro-expressions that she's being genuine.

t_ch: 况且，身为天外来客，又为奥赫玛付出了这么多心血，你们已经是大家心目中的大英雄了——阿格莱雅女士绝不会把大家晾在{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}眼皮底下烤的！
t_en: Not only are you guests from beyond the sky, but you've also done so much for Okhema. You're practically heroes to us all! Lady Aglaea would never subject you to {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila's{RUBY_E#} judgment.

t_ch: 嗯？您是……
t_en: Hmm? You are...?

t_ch: 你来了，风堇。这些日子辛苦你照顾伤者了。
t_en: Hyacine. Thank you for taking care of the wounded over the past few days.

t_ch: 不辛苦！伤员们都很配合治疗，能让大家健健康康回家去，我就心满意足啦。
t_en: It's no trouble at all! The patients are very cooperative when we attend to them. I'm just thrilled to see them all going home healthy again.

t_ch: 呀，蝶宝也在呀！想死你了。
t_en: And ooh! It's Cassie! I've missed you!

t_ch: 这位是？
t_en: Who is this...?

t_ch: 灰宝？丹宝？
t_en: Grayie? Dannie?

t_ch: 是昵称！怎么样，可爱吧？
t_en: Aren't they just the cutest nicknames? Hehe.

t_ch: 蝶宝？
t_en: Cassie?

t_ch: 风堇小姐，那个外号……
t_en: Um, Miss Hyacine, about that nickname... Could you—

t_ch: 别害羞嘛，明明很可爱~
t_en: Aw, don't be shy. It's a cute nickname!

t_ch: 怎么不管阿格莱雅叫阿宝？
t_en: Is Aglaea's nickname Aglie?
Hehe...

t_ch: 阁下…！
t_en: Mis—!

t_ch: 呀…大胆，大胆！阿格莱雅得叫女士啦！
t_en: Wah! You can't say that! Lady Aglaea should always be "Lady Aglaea"!

t_ch: 这位女孩…还真是自来熟。
t_en: This girl... She's a natural at making friends.

t_ch: 让我想起一位粉色头发的故人。
t_en: She reminds me of an old friend with pink hair.

t_ch: 俺也一样。
t_en: So am I!

t_ch: 那…交给你了？
t_en: I'll let you take it from here, then.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，毕竟我是{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}的辅祭嘛，用阳光治愈大家就是我的责任。而且而且，我还听说，两位也是遥远天空的孩子，我们可能还挺有缘分的呢。
t_en: Hehe, as an acolyte of {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#}, I'm here to bring healing light to the world. Plus, I've heard you're also children of the distant skies! Fate must have brought us together!

t_ch: 所以…我想和两位交朋友，听两位讲有关天空的故事！阿格莱雅女士，奥赫玛好不容易拿出这么珍贵的礼物，就让我也沾沾光吧？
t_en: Can we be friends? I'd love to hear your tales of the sky! And ooh, it's a rare occasion that Okhema presents such an invaluable gift to guests. Lady Aglaea, may I have the honor of tasting it, too?

t_ch: 当然，我不会拒绝。也请二位相信，这份礼物的背后并无过多深意——结盟一事，我们可以择日再议。
t_en: Of course you may. Esteemed guests of Okhema, please believe me when I say that this gift carries no hidden agendas. As for the matter of the alliance, you need not give me an answer here and now.

t_ch: 话都说到这份上了，那就…
t_en: Well, if you insist...

t_ch: 噢，合着是阿格莱雅搬的救兵。
t_en: Ohh, she's here to help Aglaea recruit us.

t_ch: 才不是，阿格莱雅女士这么厉害，我哪里能在这种事上帮得了忙。
t_en: Haha, you're mistaken! The capable Lady Aglaea doesn't need my help with things like this.

t_ch: 既然如此…那我们也不推辞了。感谢奥赫玛的赠礼，也欢迎风堇女士随时交流。
t_en: ...I see. In that case, we will accept this gift from Okhema. We welcome the chance to talk with you, Lady Hyacine.

t_ch: 好耶！那我要好好设计一下调查问卷了……
t_en: Yay! Then I'll get started on my questionnaire!

t_ch: 恕我冒昧，但从刚才起我就想问：「微表情」、「语调」、「调查问卷」…{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}的祭司竟需要掌握这些技能么？
t_en: Pardon the question, but I've been meaning to ask this for a while: micro-expressions, tone of voice, questionnaires... Are these all part of the usual skill set for {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila's{RUBY_E#} priests?

t_ch: 正式引荐一下吧——这位是来自神悟树庭「智种学派」的讲师助理，也是医疗机构「昏光庭院」的首席护理师，风堇。
t_en: Allow me to do the honors. This young lady is Hyacine, assistant lecturer of the Nousporists at the Grove of Epiphany and head nurse at the medical institution known as the Twilight Courtyard.

t_ch: 前些日子奥赫玛遭{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}袭城，不少黄金裔因战负伤，树庭特遣风堇女士前来支援奥赫玛，为伤者提供治疗……
t_en: Many of the Chrysos Heirs suffered injuries during {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s attack on Okhema, so the Grove sent Lady Hyacine to treat the wounded in Okhema.

t_ch: 但我最擅长的，还是治愈大家内心深处的症结——如果遇到麻烦了，欢迎随时来找我倾诉！只要在庭院里晒晒太阳，烦恼自然就会消失啦。
t_en: But what I'm really good at is helping people sort out what ails their mind and spirit. If you're ever feeling troubled, come find me! A little sunbathing in the courtyard will chase all your worries away!

t_ch: 原来是心理医生…我们会的，谢谢你。
t_en: Ah, so you're a therapist. Thank you, we will keep that in mind.

t_ch: 看来大家都解开心结了。阿格莱雅女士，你托我照顾的伤者也都已痊愈，我也该动身啦。
t_en: Well, it looks like everything's sorted out now! The patients have also all recovered. I think it's time for me to head back, Lady Aglaea.

t_ch: 虽然很想再多留你几日，但若将你强留在奥赫玛…树庭的人可要背后多话了。
t_en: I would love to keep you in Okhema for a few more days, but I would hate to provoke the ire of the Grove.

t_ch: 正好，奥赫玛打算遣派使者前往树庭。求取黑潮相关研究进展的同时，也要传达这道信息：就回收{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}火种一事，望能尽快讨论。
t_en: As it happens, Okhema intends to send a messenger to the Grove for updates about the research on the black tide. We also want to communicate our desire to discuss the retrieval of {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces'{RUBY_E#} Coreflame at the earliest convenience.

t_ch: 这…确实是好严肃的事情啊。阿格莱雅女士，不会是想让我……
t_en: That... sounds pretty serious. Lady Aglaea, you can't be asking me to...

t_ch: 不必紧张，我们绝不会为难你。缇安会作为外交使者前往，另外，遐蝶也会同去。
t_en: No need to fret. We won't put you on the spot. Trianne will be going there as the diplomatic messenger of Okhema, and Castorice will be accompanying her.

t_ch: 有关失踪的泰坦{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}…我恰好有些事想要请教<color=#dbc291ff>那刻夏老师</color>。
t_en: I happen to have a few questions for <color=#dbc291ff>Professor Anaxa</color> as well, regarding the missing Titan {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 既然事涉奥赫玛邦交事务，我们就先行离开了。
t_en: Since this involves Okhema's diplomatic affairs, we'll give you some privacy.

t_ch: 我要出去玩！
t_en: Let's go out and play!

t_ch: 又要回房间睡觉？好无聊哦…
t_en: Back to bed again? Boring!

t_ch: 我倒是有个提议。{NICKNAME}若闲不下来，何不与大家同去树庭？那里的景貌幽邃雅致，适合旅行。
t_en: If you're not keen on just sitting around, {NICKNAME}, why not go with them? The Grove is a picturesque place, perfect for sightseeing.

t_ch: 这个提议好，欢迎灰宝来树庭做客！就由我来做你的导游吧？
t_en: That's a wonderful suggestion! Come visit the Grove, Grayie! I'd be delighted to show you around!

t_ch: 听起来不错。多收集翁法罗斯不同地区的风土人情，也是「开拓」的一环。
t_en: Sounds good. Gaining more insight into the customs and geography of different regions of Amphoreus is also part of Trailblazing.

t_ch: 既然如此，还请各位也携着<color=#dbc291ff>这柄纺锤</color>一道出发——
t_en: In that case, do bring <color=#dbc291ff>this spindle</color> along with you.

t_ch: 此物名为<color=#dbc291ff>「引纬」</color>，是我家族世代相传的纺衣器具，如今象征意义已远大于实用性。见到此物便等于见过我本人，倘若树庭有为难的意思，出示之便可。
t_en: This tool, called the <color=#dbc291ff>Weft</color>, is used to spin thread and has been passed down in my family through generations. Though it doesn't see much practical use nowadays, it's become a symbol of great significance. In this respect, it is the perfect token to represent my will. If you encounter trouble at the Grove, just take this out and show it to them.

t_ch: 此外，遐蝶知道，在树庭的地界上，它引出的金丝能为各位勾勒周遭一切已形或未形之物，如同…我之所见。
t_en: While you're in the Grove, the golden thread of the Weft can also render all things around you visible, regardless of whether they're corporeal or not, allowing you to see things as I would. Castorice knows this, too.

t_ch: 善加运用吧。缇安在奥赫玛的城郊等待各位——祝你们此行顺利。
t_en: Make good use of it. Trianne is already waiting for you at the outskirts of Okhema, so I won't keep you any longer. May your journey be smooth.

t_ch: 能和{NICKNAME}阁下再度同行，真是太好了。
t_en: I'm glad to be in your company again, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 和我讲讲那位泰坦，塞纳托斯。
t_en: Tell me about the Titan known as Thanatos.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}…是我的命运。我身上所负的诅咒，正是来自于它。
t_en: {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}... controls my fate, in a sense. The curse I bear came from them.

t_ch: 我有重要之物…必须向它讨回。
t_en: And they have something of mine... something of great importance that I must get back from them.

t_ch: 谁是那刻夏老师？
t_en: Who is Professor Anaxa?

t_ch: 我和白厄等人曾在树庭求学，那刻夏老师当时负责教授我们与黄金裔和泰坦相关的知识。我也是在那时结识了风堇小姐——她曾是那刻夏老师的助教。
t_en: When I was studying at the Grove alongside Phainon and the others, Professor Anaxa was responsible for teaching us about the Chrysos Heirs and the Titans. That's also when I met Miss Hyacine... She was Anaxa's teaching assistant then.

t_ch: 神悟树庭是什么样的地方？
t_en: What is the Grove of Epiphany?

t_ch: 神悟树庭是奥赫玛的友邦学院——他们保管着{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的火种，是研究黑潮、泰坦等神秘事物的前沿机构，也为逐火之旅提供学术研究支持。
t_en: The Grove of Epiphany is Okhema's partner academy. They look after the Coreflame of {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} and are the organization at the forefront of researching phenomena such as the black tide and the Titans. They also provide academic research support for the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: 在那里同样驻扎着几名黄金裔，其中就有风堇小姐和那刻夏老师。
t_en: Several Chrysos Heirs are based in the Grove, including Miss Hyacine and Professor Anaxa.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了，出发！
t_en: I don't have any more questions. Let's go!

t_ch: 当时还有一名「昏光庭院」的同伴与我同来，我已经让她提前整理好行李在城门口等待了。我们去跟她会合吧！
t_en: I came here with a colleague from the Twilight Courtyard. I've asked her to pack her luggage in advance, so she's already waiting for us by the city gates. Let's go meet up with her!

t_ch: 在风堇的带领下，你们向约定地点出发……
t_en: You head towards the agreed location under Hyacine's lead...

t_ch: 发生什么事了吗，克莱门汀…啊，好重的伤！
t_en: Did something happen, Clementine? Ah... that's a serious injury!

t_ch: 风堇小姐，这位伤者是……
t_en: Miss Hyacine, this man was—

t_ch: …是缇安发现的！缇安今天早上心情不好，就跑到奥赫玛的郊外散心，然后，然后就发现他倒在了路边！
t_en: ...Was discovered by Trianne! Trianne was in a baaaad mood this morning, so we went to the outskirts of Okhema for some fresh air... b—but then we found him collapsed on the roadside!

t_ch: 缇安知道，城里有好医生！所以缇安把陌生的朋友带进城里，找好医生！
t_en: Trianne knows the city has good doctors! So Trianne brought our new friend into the city to look for a doctor!

t_ch: …我原本在这里等待风堇小姐，正巧撞见了缇安大人。当时这位伤者的情况已经十分危急…我只能先做些应急处理。
t_en: ...I was waiting here for Miss Hyacine when I bumped into Lady Trianne. By then, this man was already in critical condition... so I had to perform some emergency treatment first.

t_ch: 这是…黑潮造成的伤痕？
t_en: These wounds... They're caused by the black tide!

t_ch: 情况不妙…克莱门汀，去安排一间安静的病房吧，我来治疗——被黑潮伤成这样，能撑到现在已经是个奇迹了。
t_en: This is bad... Clementine, prepare a ward for this man at once. I'll treat him myself... It's a miracle he's still alive, considering how badly the black tide injured him.

t_ch: 灰宝、蝶宝、还有缇安老师，麻烦你们先行赶赴树庭吧？待伤者情况转好，我会和克莱门汀赶上你们的。
t_en: Grayie, Cassie, and Trianne. Would you mind making your way to the Grove first? Once this man is out of danger, Clementine and I will join you.

t_ch: 病患就在眼前…我必须忠于医者的本职。
t_en: As a healer, I cannot ignore those in need of medical attention.

t_ch: …我明白了。愿命运为这位不幸的人网开一面。
t_en: I understand. May fate have mercy on this poor man.

t_ch: 缇安也，相信他一定会好转的！
t_en: Trianne believes he'll get better!

t_ch: 很抱歉临时丢下你们…但我毕竟是一名医者，我不能放任生命在眼前逝去。
t_en: I'm sorry for leaving in the middle of the journey... but as a healer, I can't just stand by and watch someone pass away in front of me.

t_ch: 请你们先赶赴树庭吧——愿瑟希斯捍卫你们的思想！
t_en: Please make your way to the Grove first. May Cerces safeguard your thoughts!

t_ch: 从这条路，飞起来，我们就要，离开奥赫玛！
t_en: Down this road we go! To the skies we fly! Leaving Okhema behind!

t_ch: 小小蝶，小小灰，准备好了吗？
t_en: Little Cas, Little Gray, are you ready?

t_ch: 出发吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 嗯，我也准备好了。
t_en: I'm ready, too.

t_ch: 好喔，我们，大手拉小手！去树庭！
t_en: Okie-dokie! Everybody, let's hold hands! To the Grove we go!

t_ch: 为什么不直接用「百界门」？
t_en: Are we not using the Century Gate?

t_ch: 阁下大概有所不知…缇安大人每次使用「百界门」，对身体都是很大的伤害。
t_en: You might not be aware of this... but using the Century Gate takes a significant toll on Lady Trianne.

t_ch: 嗯！缇安答应过缇宝，不到万不得已，不会开门！
t_en: Mm-hmm! Trianne promised Tribbie that we wouldn't open the gate unless we had no other choice!

t_ch: 再等我一下。
t_en: Hang on a minute.

t_ch: 树庭，路途远！准备，要做好！
t_en: The Grove is far! It's important to be ready!

t_ch: 几日后 神悟树庭
t_en: A few days later, Grove of Epiphany
Hm...

t_ch: 把宝石和羊毛毡缝在一起，比我想得要难一点啊……
t_en: It's harder than I thought to sew gemstones onto wool felt...

t_ch: 小蝶真是心灵手巧，人家也想学……
t_en: Cas has such nimble fingers. I want to learn too...

t_ch: 迷迷也想试试吗？那…换你来？
t_en: Hehe, Mem wants to try sewing? Here, why don't you give it a go?

t_ch: 你们这么熟了？
t_en: How are you two so close already?

t_ch: 都这么多天啦，我和蝶已经是好朋友了！
t_en: Cas and I are now good friends after traveling together for so many days!

t_ch: 毕竟这小家伙很可爱呀。
t_en: It's hard not to like Mem when they're so adorable.

t_ch: 让我也加入吧。
t_en: I wanna try, too.

t_ch: 当然可以。
t_en: Sure.

t_ch: 那你先来，我给你加油！
t_en: You go first! I'll cheer you on!

t_ch: 说来也真是神奇…{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}一战后，它的声音仿佛就有了灵性。
t_en: This is amazing... It seems as if their voice has become more intelligible after the battle with {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}...

t_ch: 这几日相处下来，我好像也渐渐能听懂它话中的含义了…虽然只是一小部分。
t_en: After spending the last few days together, I feel like I'm gradually gaining the ability to understand what they're saying... even if it's just a little.

t_ch: 说明人家正在成长哦！有没有可能，等我拥有了戳羊毛毡的记忆，就会长出和小蝶一样漂亮的手……
t_en: This shows that I'm learning new things! Once I obtain the memory of poking wool felt, will I grow hands that are as pretty as Cas' hands?

t_ch: 这画面听着有点抽象了。
t_en: That just sounds weird.

t_ch: 蝶，求你别教它，我怕。
t_en: Scary. Don't teach her how to sew, Cas.

t_ch: 太失礼啦！怎么能对女孩子说这种话呢？
t_en: That's horrible! You can't say that to a girl!
Hehe...

t_ch: 开个玩笑！不过，人家相信，这次来神悟树庭，一定也会收获满满。
t_en: I was just kidding! Anyway, I believe that this trip to the Grove of Epiphany will definitely be a fruitful one.

t_ch: 真期待呀…不知道人家又会产生怎样的变化？
t_en: How exciting... I wonder what changes I'll experience next?

t_ch: 获得强力的新技能！
t_en: You'll learn a powerful new ability!

t_ch: 对，不仅外表可爱，还要走实力派路线！
t_en: Mm-hmm! Being adorable isn't enough. I need to be strong, too!

t_ch: 变得…更加毛茸茸？
t_en: You might... become fluffier?

t_ch: 这么喜欢可爱的小动物吗？莫非…你是很在意外貌的类型？
t_en: Do you like cute little animals that much? ...Are you secretly the type that cares a lot about outward appearances?

t_ch: 变成美少女！给我变！
t_en: Transform into a cute girl! Now!

t_ch: 哎呀，你喊那么大声干嘛啦！
t_en: Wah, you don't have to yell!

t_ch: 呼…大家已经醒了吗？小小灰，小小蝶，还有…粉色小狗？
t_en: Phew... Is everybody awake? Little Gray, Little Cas, and... a little pink puppy?

t_ch: 小…狗…？怎么连物种都变了！不应该是小小咪吗？
t_en: Puppy? I've become a different species altogether...!? And here I thought she would just give me a nickname like Little Memmy...

t_ch: 已是{RUBY_B#午时}践行时{RUBY_E#}了，缇安大人休息得如何？
t_en: It's already {RUBY_B#Noon}Action Hour{RUBY_E#}. Lady Trianne, I hope you're well-rested?

t_ch: 听你的梦话，想来是做了个漫长的梦吧？
t_en: You were talking in your sleep... Did you dream of many things?

t_ch: 唔，梦话？缇安说了什么？
t_en: Sleep talking? Um... What did Trianne say?

t_ch: 嗯…「妈妈~拿剑的娃娃~并肩作战~要勇敢！」之类的？
t_en: Something like... "Mommy! Doll with sword! Let's fight together! We'll be brave!"

t_ch: 噫，好羞人…大家不要笑话缇安！
t_en: Eek, so embarrassing...! Please don't make fun of Trianne!

t_ch: 当然不会了。
t_en: Of course not.

t_ch: 记得缇安大人的母亲，也是雅努萨波利斯的圣女吧？
t_en: Wasn't Lady Trianne's mother also the Holy Maiden of Janusopolis?

t_ch: 嗯，而且是门城有史以来最最厉害的圣女！就是妈妈为*我们*带来了救世预言——还有像红蘑菇一样可爱的红头发！
t_en: Uh-huh! Mama was the strongest Holy Maiden in all of our history! She also gave us the Prophecy of Deliverance... and she had red hair that looked like a cute red mushroom!

t_ch: 不过…缇安好像记不起妈妈的样子了。刚才的梦里也是……
t_en: But... Trianne can't remember what Mama looked like anymore... Not even in the dream just now...

t_ch: 既然这样…{NICKNAME}和人家就来助小缇安一臂之力吧！
t_en: In that case... {NICKNAME} and I will lend Trianne a hand!

t_ch: 你的超级英雄能力是什么？
t_en: So, what's your superpower?

t_ch: 可你没有胳膊，只有…肉垫。
t_en: But you don't have hands... Only paws.

t_ch: 这只是个比喻啦，比喻。
t_en: It's just a figure of speech!

t_ch: 大家想呀，这世界上到处都留下了许多回忆，里面肯定也有小缇安妈妈的身影。
t_en: Think about it: There are so many memories scattered all over this world! Surely at least one contains an image of Trianne's mama?

t_ch: 只要人家在寻找记忆的旅途中留心寻找，然后让{NICKNAME}「嘭」的一下——小缇安不光能想起妈妈的模样，甚至还能和她对话了，没错吧？
t_en: If I keep a lookout for it while searching for memories along our journey, then {NICKNAME} can go "boom"... and not only will Trianne be able to remember what her mama looks like, she'll even be able to talk with her! Right?

t_ch: 唔…缇安觉得这个主意很棒！那，缇安可以拜托粉色小狗嘛？
t_en: Hmm... Trianne thinks that's a great idea! Can the pink puppy help Trianne out?

t_ch: …只要小缇安别再叫粉色小狗，人家就答应你哦！
t_en: Only if you promise to stop calling me a pink puppy!

t_ch: 好！那以后就叫你——小小咪！
t_en: Yay! Then I'll call you... Little Memmy from now on!

t_ch: 嗯！这还差不多…<size=-4>不过，虽然刚才随口说了个名字，但人家到底是什么物种？</size>
t_en: That's much better... <size=-4>Still, I'd like to know what exactly I am. That was just a name I came up with on a whim...</size>

t_ch: 算了，不管是什么，人家说话算话！既然小缇安打起精神了，那我们出发吧？
t_en: Ah, well... It doesn't matter, because I'll be sticking to my word anyway. Now that Trianne has cheered up, shall we get going?

t_ch: 好！缇安记得，*我们*要帮阿雅去问问大树，{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}愿不愿意去奥赫玛做客……
t_en: Okie-dokie! Trianne remembers that we need to help Agy ask the Big Tree whether {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} would be willing to visit Okhema...

t_ch: 沿着这条经纬小径向上，很快就能抵达树庭啦。
t_en: If we follow this Woven Trail and keep going upward, we'll reach the Grove in no time.

t_ch: 「经纬小径」…这名字有什么含义吗？
t_en: Woven Trail... Where did it get its name from?

t_ch: 传说中，{RUBY_B#浪漫之泰坦}墨涅塔{RUBY_E#}求见瑟希斯的时候，总会穿上白纱织的长裙。经线和纬线落在地上，就变成了道路和台阶……
t_en: Legend has it that whenever they requested an audience with Cerces, {RUBY_B#Romance Titan}Mnestia{RUBY_E#} would always come to the meeting in a long white dress. As the warps and wefts of the dress came into contact with the ground, they turned into paths and stairs...

t_ch: 愿这条浪漫之路，能为奥赫玛带去佳音。
t_en: May this romantic path bring good tidings to Okhema.

t_ch: 巨树在无声的林间微微颔首，斜着身形，倒伏在萎靡的翠浪中。偶然掠过的风穿林打叶，好似叹息颤响，幽幽落入天上无光的海洋。
t_en: A giant tree bowed slightly in the silent forest, tilting and falling into the wilting green waves. A passing breeze rustled through the leaves, as if it was sighing, softly fading into the lightless ocean above on high.

t_ch: 三月七会喜欢这番绝景么？你有些拿不准；丹恒或许……
t_en: Would March 7th appreciate this sublime scene? You're uncertain. Dan Heng, perhaps...

t_ch: 算了，他两辈子遇见这么大的树时，都处于万分头疼的境地。
t_en: Never mind, every time he encounters a tree as big as this one, it would be a deeply troubling situation.

t_ch: 拍摄很大的树。
t_en: Take a photo of this big tree.

t_ch: （这照片真该得个奖…不愧是我。）
t_en: (I never fail to impress... This photo deserves an award!)

t_ch: 不过，这棵巨大的树究竟是……
t_en: What is this huge tree, though...?

t_ch: 那就是{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}裂分之枝，瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的神躯。它所扎根之处，也就是我们脚下的平原盆地，花叶树木开始生长，并逐渐形成了一片规模可观的蓊郁森林。
t_en: It's the {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Bough of Rift{RUBY_E#} — the divine body of Cerces. Flora began to grow where they planted their roots, and this flatland basin upon which we stand gradually became a large, verdant forest.

t_ch: 很久以前，「最初的学者」塞勒苏斯带领众人在林间建立起花园与庭院，并取名「神悟树庭」，供世人醒神、求识、悟道之用。
t_en: A long time ago, Thalesus, the First Scholar, led the masses to build a garden and courtyard in the forest, naming the site the "Grove of Epiphany." There, people sought mental clarity, knowledge, and enlightenment.

t_ch: 这个缇安知道！大家说，这座很大的学院，就是这么来的。
t_en: Trianne knows this! Everybody says that was how this big academy came to be.

t_ch: 不过，学院是这么安静的地方吗？
t_en: But... is the academy supposed to be this quiet?

t_ch: …总觉得有哪里不对劲。
t_en: Something feels off...

t_ch: 树庭历来欢迎求学者，{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}也会借微风捎来嫩叶，并伸出枝梢，结成道路，以示迎接。
t_en: The Grove has always welcomed scholars. So too would {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} give us her delicate leaves and extend her branches in greeting.

t_ch: 可现在…树庭的使者也好，泰坦也好，似乎都无动于衷。
t_en: Yet... it seems both the Grove's messengers and the Titan are indifferent to our arrival.

t_ch: 那我们岂不是没办法进去了？
t_en: So, we have no way in?

t_ch: 应该还有办法。我只是…有些惊讶。
t_en: There should still be a way in. I'm just... a little surprised.

t_ch: 树庭闭门谢客了？
t_en: Is the Grove closed to visitors?

t_ch: 不应该…吧。毕竟，风堇小姐也没有提到过这件事。
t_en: That doesn't seem likely... Miss Hyacine didn't mention anything about it.

t_ch: 这泰坦太不懂事了！
t_en: What an inconsiderate Titan!

t_ch: 阁下，这种话…等下进去以后可千万不要说。
t_en: Um... Please don't speak such words once we're in the confines of the Grove.

t_ch: 或许，大家只是因为还没上课，在睡大觉！怕别人打扰，就把门关上了。
t_en: Maybe everybody's still asleep because classes haven't started yet! They don't want to be disturbed, so they shut the entrance.

t_ch: 无论如何…我们先在附近转转，探探情况吧。
t_en: For now... let's look around and see if we can find out what's happened.

t_ch: 果然不对劲……
t_en: ...As I expected, something's not right here.

t_ch: 即便不是求学者，都已经走到这里了，还是没见人出来接引我们呀……
t_en: Even though we're not scholars, someone should've come forward to receive us by now...

t_ch: 四周也安静得有些可怕了。
t_en: Not to mention that this whole place is also eerily quiet.

t_ch: 也许阿格莱雅没提前知会他们？
t_en: Maybe Aglaea didn't give them a heads-up?

t_ch: 如此重要的外交事务，她绝不可能怠慢。
t_en: There is no way she would have neglected such an important diplomatic matter.

t_ch: 也许…出事了？
t_en: Maybe... something happened?

t_ch: 嗯…不排除这个可能性。
t_en: Hm... That's possible.

t_ch: 也许…他们真的在睡大觉？
t_en: Maybe they really are asleep?

t_ch: 不太可能…在我记忆里，树庭里的辩论声终日不绝于耳。
t_en: That's not very likely... As far as I can recall, the voices of people engaged in debate echoed throughout the Grove at all hours of the day.

t_ch: <size=36>有——人——吗——？</size>
t_en: <size=36>Helloooo? Is anyone thereeee?</size>

t_ch: 没有！
t_en: No one's here!

t_ch: 吓我一跳！
t_en: Whoa, that scared me!

t_ch: 现在怎么办？
t_en: What should we do now?

t_ch: 要不打道回府？
t_en: Should we head back?

t_ch: 真是的…别这么快放弃，至少也像只小兔子一样挣扎一下吧。
t_en: Come now... Don't give up so easily! At least struggle a bit like a scared bunny!

t_ch: …对了。缇安大人，那支<color=#dbc291ff>金色的纺锤</color>，还请借我一用。
t_en: ...I have an idea. Lady Trianne, could you lend me that <color=#dbc291ff>golden spindle</color>?

t_ch: 还记得吗？阿格莱雅大人说过：「在树庭的地界上，它引出的金丝也能为各位勾勒周遭一切已形或未形之物」……
t_en: Do you remember? Lady Aglaea said this: "While you're in the Grove, the golden thread of the Weft can also render all things around you visible, regardless of whether they're corporeal or not."

t_ch: 这是因为，树庭并不单是{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的根系所在，也是{RUBY_B#浪漫之泰坦}「黄金之茧」{RUBY_E#}显迹之地——
t_en: This is achievable because the Grove is not only where the roots of {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} are... but also where the Chrysalis of Gold manifests.

t_ch: 「{RUBY_B#浪漫之泰坦}墨涅塔{RUBY_E#}呵！以『引纬』封证誓约，我向你求愿——愿你的金丝与爱缠绕我身！」
t_en: "O {RUBY_B#Romance Titan}Mnestia{RUBY_E#}! With the Weft as my vow, I beseech you: Entwine my body with your golden thread and affection!"

t_ch: 你也吓我一跳！
t_en: Whoa, that scared me too!

t_ch: 对不起…为了让金丝与地脉中的泰坦显迹共鸣，必须得念这个。
t_en: Ah, I'm sorry... I had to recite that chant so that the golden thread would resonate with the Titan's presence within the leylines.

t_ch: 这是什么仪式祷文吗？
t_en: Is this some kind of ritualistic prayer?

t_ch: 这个…是墨涅塔祭司手册里记载的箴言。
t_en: This is... a proverb that was recorded in the handbook of Mnestia's priests.

t_ch: 这谁想的台词，真抽象…
t_en: Who came up with this? How... unique...

t_ch: 这个…墨涅塔祭司手册里就是这么记载的……
t_en: Well... These lines are documented in the handbook of Mnestia's priests, so...

t_ch: 如果不错…我们应当能借金丝从这枚蝶蜕中汲取力量。
t_en: If I'm not mistaken... we should be able to draw power out of this butterfly exuvia with the golden thread.

t_ch: 唔，好像没有反应…是不是没起效果呀？
t_en: Ahh, there isn't a reaction... Did it not work?

t_ch: 怎么会？不应该呀……
t_en: But that shouldn't be...

t_ch: 咦，大家快看，那边——有什么东西冒出来了？
t_en: Eh? Everybody, look over there! Something's appearing...

t_ch: 这是…仙女木？
t_en: Is that... a White Dryad?

t_ch: 仙女木…这些小家伙，名字还真可爱。
t_en: White Dryad... What a cute name.

t_ch: 不对，这不是仙女木……
t_en: No, it isn't a White Dryad...

t_ch: 这是死去仙女木的影子。
t_en: It is the shadow of a dead White Dryad.

t_ch: 死去仙女木……
t_en: Dead White Dryad...

t_ch: …的影子？
t_en: ...Shadow?

t_ch: 它们…严格来说是植物，因{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}平等的赐福获得了智能，但相较人类十分有限。眼前这些小家伙已经死去了…只留下灵魂一道小小的残影。
t_en: Strictly speaking... these white dryads are still plants. They only gained sentience as a result of {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}' blessing of equality, but their sentience is minimal when compared to that of humans. These flowers have already died... What you see here are just faint afterimages of their souls.

t_ch: 不过，这也意味着<color=#dbc291ff>金丝</color>确实与泰坦显迹系于一身了——没有金丝勾勒，我们绝无可能看到它们。
t_en: However, this also means that <color=#dbc291ff>the golden thread</color> has definitely connected with the Titan's presence. Without the golden thread's outlines, we would not have been able to see them at all.

t_ch: 那…它们到底遭遇了什么？为什么只剩下小小的影子了？
t_en: But... what happened to them? Why are there only tiny shadows of them left?

t_ch: 迷迷，能请你试着感应它们承载的记忆么？
t_en: Mem, can you read their memories?

t_ch: 人家试试看。迷…迷迷……
t_en: Let me try. Mem... memem...

t_ch: 有说法吗？
t_en: Got anything?

t_ch: 马上……
t_en: Almost...

t_ch: 迷迷…迷迷迷…
t_en: Memem... mememem...

t_ch: 哎呀，你不要和人家一起用力，分心啦…！
t_en: Hey! Don't say it together with me! You're distracting me!

t_ch: 「黑色」…「斗篷」…还有「利剑」……
t_en: "Black"... "Cloak"... and "Sword"...

t_ch: 「向大树」……
t_en: "Toward the Great Tree"...

t_ch: …唔，没有了。它们的记忆很稀薄，连画面都没有，只能听到这几个模糊的词。
t_en: Uh... That's it. Their memories are too faint for me to see anything. I could only vaguely make out these words.

t_ch: …各位，我有种不好的预感。
t_en: ...I have a bad feeling about this.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的死雾…似乎正在迫近。
t_en: The deathly fog of {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}... is fast approaching.

t_ch: 这…好可怕……
t_en: This looks... really scary...

t_ch: 这是什么…野兽吗？还是「死亡」泰坦的眷属？
t_en: What kind of... beast is this? Or is it the Death Titan's Kin?

t_ch: 不，都不是……
t_en: No, it's neither of those...

t_ch: 这就是「黑潮」造物，生命遭到侵蚀形成的，扭曲而空虚的躯壳。
t_en: This is a creation of the black tide. Such creatures are formed when living beings are corrupted and turned into deformed, hollow husks of their former selves.

t_ch: 大家，提高警惕…无论树庭发生了什么，能在这里见到黑潮造物的遗骸，绝不是个好兆头。
t_en: Everybody, stay alert. Regardless of what has happened to the Grove, the fact that we're seeing the remains of a black tide creation here definitely doesn't bode well.

t_ch: 我们…我们要先告诉阿雅吗？
t_en: Should we... should we inform Agy about this?

t_ch: 啊，等等……
t_en: Ah, wait...!

t_ch: 那里…黑色的…好像动起来了？
t_en: That... black thing... seems to be moving?

t_ch: 怪、怪物活了！缇安来保护好大家…！
t_en: Th—The monster is alive! Trianne will protect everybody...!

t_ch: …缇安，先躲到我们身后。
t_en: ...Trianne, please hide behind us.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下，我们一起上吧。
t_en: {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}, let's do this.

t_ch: 干、干掉了！小小灰，小小蝶，好厉害……
t_en: Y—You defeated it! Little Gray, Little Cas, you're so strong...

t_ch: 可是，这些黑乎乎的家伙…不该出现在这里吧？
t_en: But these monsters... They aren't supposed to show up here, right?

t_ch: 虽然树庭所处地理位置相对偏僻，但黑潮竟能迅速蔓延至此……
t_en: To think the black tide has spread this quickly to the Grove despite its remote location...

t_ch: 「纷争」失守的世界，恐怕要比我们预想得更凶险。
t_en: I'm afraid a world where Strife has fallen is far more dangerous than we ever imagined.

t_ch: 呜，那奥赫玛岂不是也危险了？
t_en: Oh no... Does this mean that Okhema is also in danger?

t_ch: 单看这一地狼藉…学者们应该至少挡住了第一波进攻。
t_en: Looking at this mess... it seems the scholars at least succeeded in fending off the first wave of attacks.

t_ch: 只是，不知道付出了多少代价……
t_en: But... at what price?

t_ch: 与此同时 奥赫玛
t_en: At the same time, in Okhema

t_ch: 阿格莱雅女士，那名出现在奥赫玛城郊的伤患，经过几日大小手术，已经性命无虞了。
t_en: Lady Aglaea, after a few days of surgeries both major and minor, we were able to save the life of that patient found on the outskirts of Okhema.

t_ch: 平白又为你添了辛劳，风堇。
t_en: Thank you again, Hyacine, for your hard work.

t_ch: 他受黑潮影响，脏器的纤维化尤为严重，能活下来已经是奇迹，我不能保证他何时能够醒来……
t_en: The scarring of his organs was especially severe, a result of the black tide's corruption... It's a miracle he's even survived. I can't say for sure when he'll regain consciousness...

t_ch: 别有压力，你已尽了你的职责。现在也别着急回树庭了，先好好休息吧。
t_en: Don't stress yourself out over this. You've already done everything in your power to save him. There's no need to hurry to the Grove either. You deserve a good rest now.

t_ch: 吾师，我还有些事想与你说。那名伤者的来历…有些蹊跷。
t_en: Tribbie, I have some things to tell you as well... There's something strange about where that patient came from.

t_ch: 他并非奥赫玛人。其身上所着衣裳，针脚的藏埋方式颇为特殊…是树庭的手艺。
t_en: He is not Okheman. The way the stitches on his clothes are hidden is quite unique... Such craftsmanship is undeniably from the Grove.

t_ch: 树庭？难道说……
t_en: The Grove? You mean...

t_ch: 算算时间，遐蝶她们也该到了。
t_en: Now that I think about it, Castorice and the rest should have arrived at the Grove by now.

t_ch: 啊…缇安来消息了！
t_en: Ah! Trianne just got in contact!
Mmm...

t_ch: *我们*…*我们*看到树庭已遭黑潮造物入侵……
t_en: The black tide has invaded the Grove...

t_ch: 透过金线，也暂时还没有感知到幸存者的存在…怎、怎么办，阿雅？
t_en: We haven't detected any survivors, even with the help of the golden thread... Wh—What should we do, Agy?

t_ch: …黑潮威胁果真加剧了，只没想到竟是树庭首当其冲。
t_en: ...The black tide is posing an even greater risk to us now. I did not expect the Grove to be the first to be impacted, though.

t_ch: {M#他们}{F#她们}能感知到火种如今身在何方么？
t_en: Can they tell where the Coreflame is right now?
Mmm...

t_ch: *我们*看到了…火种还在，只是……
t_en: We see it... The Coreflame is still there, but...

t_ch: 只是？
t_en: But what?

t_ch: 只是…{M#他们}{F#她们}无法确认泰坦有没有也受到黑潮感染……
t_en: But... they can't say for sure whether the Titan has also been corrupted by the black tide.
Ah...

t_ch: 当务之急是尽快确认并转移火种，搜寻幸存者，以便厘清树庭之灾始末…就这么转告{M#他们}{F#她们}吧。
t_en: Please let them know that for now, the most pressing task is to identify the Coreflame and transfer it to a safe place as quickly as possible. We must also search for survivors and find out exactly what happened to the Grove.

t_ch: 还有——务必确认<color=#dbc291ff>那刻夏</color>的下落。如无意外，他一定还活着。
t_en: Oh, and... find <color=#dbc291ff>Anaxa</color>. Unless something went terribly wrong, he should still be alive.

t_ch: 她们出发了。但愿大家平安无事……
t_en: They've set off. Please, stay safe, everyone...

t_ch: …但愿吧。
t_en: I pray for their safety, too.

t_ch: 不过，阿雅…你怎么知道那刻夏还活着？
t_en: Agy... how do you know that Anaxa is still alive?

t_ch: 我对他的秉性再清楚不过。倘若这位渎神的「大表演家」知道自己命不久矣，定会打起火种的主意，教泰坦为自己陪葬。
t_en: I know that man all too well. If that blasphemous "performer" knew that he was not long for this world, he would most certainly try to seize the Coreflame and drag the Titan down with him.

t_ch: 这也是个…玩笑吗？
t_en: Is this... a joke as well?

t_ch: 不，这次是认真的。
t_en: No, I'm being serious this time.

t_ch: 内忧外患呵。{RUBY_B#「黄金战争」}痛苦的残杀{RUBY_E#}虽已辍停，奥赫玛的宿敌却在我们眼下砥砺锋芒至今……
t_en: We're facing threats from both within and without Okhema. The {RUBY_B#Chrysos War}merciless carnage{RUBY_E#} may have ceased, but Okhema's nemeses continue to sharpen their blades even now...

t_ch: 现在，他们或已为我们备好绞索了。
t_en: They may have even prepared the gallows from which to hang us already.

t_ch: 你说的是……
t_en: You mean...

t_ch: 即便「纷争」之神权悬置，这道黑色巨浪未免也来得太过迅猛…我猜这场惨剧不只是天灾。
t_en: Even if the divine authority of Strife is as yet unclaimed, the black tide is advancing more rapidly than expected... I fear this tragedy is more than a mere natural disaster.

t_ch: 可惜，既已事发，只怕元老院又会乘机有所动作…我会尽可能封锁消息，你也要留心，防患于未然。
t_en: Alas, it's too late. The Council will likely take this opportunity to do something... I will try to keep this under wraps for now, but you must also take care and be prepared for anything that may happen.

t_ch: 嗯。那…还要告诉小风堇么？
t_en: Alright. Should I... inform Hyacine about this?

t_ch: 晚些吧…她连日进行手术，已经疲惫不堪。我不想她被这突如其来的噩耗压垮。
t_en: Not for now... She must be exhausted after performing surgeries non-stop for so many days. I don't want to overwhelm her with this bad news.

t_ch: 唉…希望树庭的人们，度过这次难关的…越多越好。
t_en: ...We can only hope that the Grove weathers this crisis with as many survivors as possible.

t_ch: 吾师，只看这名伤患的状况，你我应做好最坏的打算……
t_en: That being said, based on the condition of this patient, Tribbie, we should prepare for the worst.

t_ch: …除他拼死逃出，树庭恐怕凶多吉少。
t_en: ...It's likely he is the only one who managed to escape from the Grove, against all odds.

t_ch: 缇安觉得…会没事的！树庭的大家都很聪明，肯定能赶跑黑潮。
t_en: Everything will be fine! The people at the Grove are really smart. They'll definitely be able to chase away the black tide!

t_ch: 而且，阿格莱雅说那位那刻夏还活着，那其他人应该也有希望……
t_en: Aglaea said that Anaxa is still alive. If that's true, then there's still hope for everyone else too...

t_ch: …不过这人到底是谁？总觉得大家对他有一种莫名的信任？
t_en: Who is this Anaxa anyway? Why does everybody seem to have some kind of trust in him?

t_ch: 怎么说呢……
t_en: How should I put it...

t_ch: 阿格莱雅大人对他的态度，恐怕截然相反吧。
t_en: Lady Aglaea doesn't "trust" Anaxa at all, I'm afraid.

t_ch: 听起来是号大人物。
t_en: He sounds like a big shot.

t_ch: 这下我完全理解了…
t_en: Ah... That makes complete sense...

t_ch: 嗯？好伙伴，你也遇到过什么麻烦吗？
t_en: Hm? Were you in a similar situation before, friend?

t_ch: 相信我，阁下。她对两位的态度远远好过那刻夏。
t_en: Believe me, she is far nicer to the two of you than she is to Anaxa.

t_ch: 那刻夏老师身为黄金裔…却坚定地认为「泰坦」和「神谕」都是一场笑话。
t_en: Despite being a Chrysos Heir, Anaxa resolutely believes that Titans and prophecies are a load of nonsense.

t_ch: 定期召开的公民大会上，对逐火之旅表决的反对票里，也总会有一票出自他之手。
t_en: During our regular citizens' assemblies, he always votes to oppose the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: 啊？这种人还在这里搞研究…你们刚才不是说，树庭是围绕泰坦建立的学院嘛？
t_en: Huh? And he's still able to conduct research here? Didn't you just say that the Grove is an academy that was established around a Titan?

t_ch: 这个，缇安知道！大家都说，象征理性的{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}，对新思想最宽容了——它是和{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}一样的，对我们最最好的泰坦。
t_en: Trianne knows the answer to that! Everybody says that {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}, who symbolizes reason, is the most tolerant of novel ideas. Apart from {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, they're the Titan who treats us the best.

t_ch: 可连这种*新思想*也…看来这位{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}确实宽容。
t_en: Well, it looks like {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} must really be open-minded if they're accepting of even this kind of novel idea.

t_ch: 嗯，因此我们和这些对人类抱有善意的泰坦达成协定：在逐火之旅中，它们会为我们提供支持，而我们也会只有在旅途的最后一刻，才会将它们的火种归位。
t_en: Yes, that is how we formed an agreement with these Titans who bear no harm toward humanity. If they agree to support us on our Flame-Chase Journey, we will wait till the final moments of the journey to restore their Coreflames.

t_ch: 除了…{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}。它是不得已才将力量交给我们的。
t_en: ...With the exception of {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}, that is. They didn't lend us their power willingly.

t_ch: 人家倒是觉得，{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}没那么勉为其难。不然它也不可能认可{NICKNAME}，还让人家和大家一起旅行啦，对不对？
t_en: I don't think {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#} was as reluctant to help us as you put it, or they wouldn't have deemed {NICKNAME} worthy and allowed me to journey alongside all of you!

t_ch: 确实。也多亏这几位尊神先前没有受到黑潮影响，陷入疯狂……
t_en: That is true. It's also fortunate that these gods weren't corrupted by the black tide and did not descend into madness...

t_ch: 缇安相信，{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}不会有事的！毕竟都说了，树庭的大家都很聪明嘛。
t_en: Trianne believes {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#} will be alright! After all, everybody says that the people of the Grove are really smart.

t_ch: 所以，小小灰，小小蝶，寻找小那刻夏和大家的任务，就交给缇安吧——缇安找人可厉害了，可以飞上天！
t_en: So, Little Gray and Little Cas, just leave the task of finding Anaxa and everyone else to Trianne! It's easy for Trianne to look for people because we can fly!

t_ch: 也好。那缇安大人带上「引纬」吧，金丝在高处应该能发挥更大作用。
t_en: Good idea. In that case, please bring the Weft along with you, Lady Trianne. The golden thread will be much more effective from up above.

t_ch: 不过，请千万答应我们…保护好自己。
t_en: Please promise us that you'll take care of yourself.

t_ch: 好！缇安遇到危险，一定会来找大家的。当然，如果大家遇到危险，也要记得呼唤缇安——缇安会赶来给大家开门！
t_en: Okie-dokie! If Trianne gets in trouble, we'll look for all of you. In return, if you get in trouble, remember to call for Trianne, okay? We'll come right over and open the Gate for you!

t_ch: 这就是我要说的第二件事了：不到万不得已，千万不要「开门」。你的身体状况并不乐观。
t_en: That's the other thing I wish to mention: Do not open the Gate unless you have no other choice, Lady Trianne. Your body is... not in good shape at the moment.

t_ch: 唔，*我们*记得的。缇安只是想帮大家……
t_en: Ah... Okay, we will keep that in mind. Trianne was just trying to help...

t_ch: 我们当然知道啦，毕竟缇安大人可是我们之中最最厉害的「半神」。
t_en: Of course we know that. After all, Lady Trianne is a demigod, the most powerful among all of us here.

t_ch: 还记得故事里勇敢的大英雄吗？他们都会把强大的力量留到最后再用，这样才能万无一失，对不对？
t_en: Remember all those brave heroes in the tales you've heard? They would always save their strength until the very last moment. That way, they could ensure that nothing went wrong with their plans. Isn't that right?

t_ch: 小小蝶，说得对！那，勇敢的缇安，先给大家开路！
t_en: Mm-hmm! In that case, brave Trianne shall clear the way for all of you!

t_ch: 小缇安真有干劲…人家也不能被落下哪。
t_en: Little Trianne is so motivated... I mustn't fall behind!

t_ch: 就用人家才有的，{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的力量，来帮各位好伙伴的忙！
t_en: I'll use something to help all of my friends that only I have: {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s power!

t_ch: 黑色的怪物业已倒下，如同倾圮的雕像。其身躯内有金色的余灰残留，与翁法罗斯常见的黄金血或为一物。
t_en: The black-colored monster has fallen like a toppled statue. Within its body lie golden ashes, perhaps the same substance as the golden blood that is commonly seen in Amphoreus.

t_ch: 迷…迷迷……
t_en: Mem... Memem...

t_ch: 不行…人家什么也没看到。
t_en: It's no good... I didn't see anything.

t_ch: 怪物的躯壳如荆棘般盘根错节，拒绝一切生命靠近。
t_en: The monster's body is twisted like brambles, not allowing any living being to approach.

t_ch: 让人家来看看……
t_en: Let me see...

t_ch: 啊…扎到人家了！而且什么都没有……
t_en: Ow, that hurt! And I didn't even detect any memories...

t_ch: 面目可憎的扭曲生命——过去也曾是一颗能为伤痛恻隐的鲜活灵魂，如今却只能对旁人锋锐相向，直到其魂息无声飘离躯壳。
t_en: A despicable, twisted life form — it was once a living soul that could mourn pain, but now it can only turn its head at others, until its soul drifts silently out of its shell.

t_ch: 这只怪物或许承载了有用的记忆……
t_en: Maybe this monster will show us some useful memories...

t_ch: 好吧…是人家想多了。
t_en: I shouldn't have gotten my hopes up.

t_ch: 呜，人家尽力了…怎么一点点记忆都看不到呢？
t_en: Ugh, I really tried my hardest... Why couldn't I see even a sliver of a memory?

t_ch: 或许是因为…被黑潮吞噬的生命，已毫无灵魂可言了吧。
t_en: It could be because when a life is devoured by the black tide... it loses its soul.

t_ch: 打不开…是为了抵御黑潮，把门封上了吧。
t_en: I can't open it... It was probably sealed in order to ward off the black tide.

t_ch: 这门也没钥匙孔啊…
t_en: There's no keyhole in this door...

t_ch: 嗯，翁法罗斯的绝大多数门都是靠{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的力量封闭的。
t_en: Yes, most of the doors on Amphoreus are sealed using the power of {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 炎枪，冲锋——！
t_en: Flaming lance, chaaaarge!

t_ch: 还是不必费力了，阁下。{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的力量…远比我们想象的更强韧。
t_en: Save your energy. {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}'s power... is far stronger than we could ever imagine.

t_ch: 既然是命运三泰坦的造物，那说不定能借{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的眼睛找到窍门……
t_en: Since this door is a creation of the Three Titans of Fate, maybe the eyes of {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#} will help me find a way to open it...

t_ch: 笑话。一扇门而已，第一时间竟想的是诉诸众神……
t_en: Are you serious? Just a mere door, and your first instinct is to call on the gods?
Memi...!?

t_ch: 打开门上的锁前，还是先设法打开眼上的锁吧。
t_en: Before you try unlocking the door, perhaps you should first find a way to clear the fog that clouds your vision.

t_ch: 吓人家一跳！你…是记忆的回声？
t_en: You scared me! Are you... the echoes of a memory?

t_ch: 我名为阿那克萨戈拉斯，神悟树庭七贤人之一，「智种学派」的创始者。丑话先说在前头：第一，别叫我那刻夏。
t_en: My name is Anaxagoras, one of the Seven Sages of the Grove of Epiphany and the founder of the Nousporists. Before we go any further: Rule number one, do not call me Anaxa.

t_ch: 阿那克…那刻…哎，算了，还是叫你那刻夏吧。现在，你能回答……
t_en: Anaxago... Anax... Ugh, I'll just stick to Anaxa. Now, can you tell me—

t_ch: 第二，别打断我——沉默是金。
t_en: Rule number two: Never interrupt me. Silence is golden.

t_ch: 这个人，好难相处呀！
t_en: What a difficult person!

t_ch: 此刻，站在这里与各位交谈的，并非记忆的残留，而是我科学的结晶，高贵、纯粹、确凿、无疑的真理。
t_en: The person standing here and conversing with you right now isn't the vestiges of a memory. Rather, it is the culmination of the greatness of science and the pure, irrefutable, and undeniable exalted truth.

t_ch: 藉「炼金」之道，我将灵魂打碎，炼成黄金，埋入此地。我录下这些话，以便来人明白自己该去向何处。
t_en: Through the wonders of alchemy, I have shattered my soul and transmuted it into gold before burying it in this spot. I have recorded these words so that visitors may learn where their next destination lies.

t_ch: 「炼金术」还能做到这种事？
t_en: Alchemy can do that?

t_ch: 一切皆有可能，但要注意，炼金并非万全之术。各位所能听到的内容取决于我付出的灵魂当量，并非无中生有。
t_en: Nothing is impossible. Having said that, alchemy is not omnipotent. Each time you hear from me, an equivalent part of my soul is being consumed as the price. In other words — this voice isn't created out of thin air.

t_ch: 怎么又接住人家的话了…你真是记忆的回声吗？
t_en: But how is it that you can respond to me...? Are you really the echoes of a memory?

t_ch: 因为我的计算纤悉无遗。我还知道各位已发现树庭因黑潮遭难，更误以为学者们已经全军覆没。
t_en: That's because my precise calculations account for every possibility. I also know that you have already discovered the cause of the calamity at the Grove — the black tide — and that you mistakenly believe that the scholars have been wiped.

t_ch: 但你们大错特错——贤人们已经在黑潮抵近前，设法将绝大部分人疏散。他们此时正在前往圣城奥赫玛的路上。
t_en: But that is where you're wrong: While the black tide was still approaching the Grove, the sages found a way to evacuate the vast majority of the scholars. They are heading toward the holy city of Okhema as we speak.

t_ch: 只有小部分学者与我一起留守，捍卫树庭。如果不出意外，{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的火种应在不久后同时抵达奥赫玛……
t_en: Only a small fraction of the scholars stayed behind with me to defend the Grove. If all goes well, {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}' Coreflame will reach Okhema before long...

t_ch: …但既然各位读取了这条消息，那就说明意外已经发生。现在，请继续前进，前往<color=#dbc291ff>启蒙王座</color>——回收火种，以及我们的遗体。
t_en: ...But since you have accessed this message, something unforeseen must have occurred. For now, you should continue onward to the <color=#dbc291ff>Luminary Throne</color> where you may retrieve the Coreflame... and our mortal remains.

t_ch: 「至是，工程已毕，言尽于此。」
t_en: "And now, the objective is complete. That is all."

t_ch: 又、又突然消失了？他说完了？
t_en: H—He disappeared again? Did he finish what he wanted to say?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下，看你们的样子…是找到有价值的记忆了么？
t_en: {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}, judging from your reaction, you must have found a useful memory.

t_ch: （复述记忆中的见闻）
t_en: (Recount what you saw in the memory)

t_ch: 那刻夏老师？信息量还真大…抱歉，请让我消化一下。
t_en: Professor Anaxa? That's a lot to digest at once... I'm sorry, please give me some time.

t_ch: 他说他叫阿那克萨戈拉斯。
t_en: He said his name is Anaxagoras.

t_ch: 这个…不用太放在心上。不知道为什么，他特别在意自己的全名。
t_en: ...You don't have to take that too seriously. For some reason, he's especially particular about his full name.

t_ch: 「智种学派」和「炼金」是…
t_en: Nousporists and alchemy refer to...

t_ch: 那刻夏老师认为万物皆诞生于同一源头，亦有相同构成，那就是灵魂。而他将其命名为「智种」……
t_en: Professor Anaxa theorizes that all beings originate from the same source and have a similar composition, which in this case refers to the soul. He calls this theory Nousporism...

t_ch: 这套理论十分复杂，如果感兴趣，不妨让他亲自讲解吧。
t_en: It is an extremely complicated theory. You should get him to explain it himself if you're interested.

t_ch: 前提是…他还活着。
t_en: Provided that... he is still alive.

t_ch: 大家似乎都被疏散走了。
t_en: Everyone seems to have been evacuated.

t_ch: 嗯，这算是到目前为止唯一一个好消息了。事已至此，我们专心回收火种就好。
t_en: Yes, that seems like the only good news so far. We can just focus on retrieving the Coreflame then.

t_ch: 那刻夏或许…还是没活下来。
t_en: Perhaps Anaxa... did not manage to survive.
